docs.title: Home
docs.text:
An easy-to-use ntfy python library.

Note

The documentation is a work in progress. If you see something missing or incorrect, please feel free to open a pull request.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: To get going with some basic functionality, see the quickstart guide.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: For examples of common use-cases, check out the examples page.

docs.title: Reference
docs.text: For a full API reference, see the reference page.

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• Github
  • PyPi

docs.title: Set up the client with no credentials
docs.text: client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

docs.title: Set up the client with username and password credentials passed to the NtfyClient constructor
docs.text: client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic", auth=("your_username", "your_password"))

docs.title: Set up the client with a bearer token passed to the NtfyClient constructor
docs.text: client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic", auth="your_token")

docs.title: Set up the client with username and password credentials from environment variables
docs.text:
environ["NTFY_USER"] = "your_username"
environ["NTFY_PASSWORD"] = "your_password"
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

docs.title: Set up the client with a bearer token from environment variables
docs.text:
environ["NTFY_TOKEN"] = "your_token"
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

docs.title: Send a basic message
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

client.send("Your message here")

docs.title: Send a markdown formatted message
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

message = """# My Message

- Bullet
- Points
"""

client.send(message, format_as_markdown=True)

docs.title: Send a message with an attachment
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

client.send("Your message here", attachment="/path/to/your/file.txt")

docs.title: Send a message with priority
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

client.send("Your message here", priority=client.MessagePriority.HIGH)

docs.title: Send a message with tags
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

client.send("Your message here", tags=["tag1", "tag2"])

docs.title: Send a message with click action
docs.text:
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

client.send("Your message here", actions=[client.ViewAction(label="Open Website", url="https://ntfy.sh"),])

docs.title: Quickstart Guide
docs.text:
1. Install using pip with pip3 install python-ntfy
  2. Import and instantiate the NtfyClient:
# Import the ntfy client
from python_ntfy import NtfyClient

# With no credentials
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")

# With credentials passed to the `NtfyClient` constructor (takes precedence over environment variables)
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic", auth=("your_username", "your_password")) # Basic auth
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic", auth="your_token") # Token auth

# Or set credentials from environment variables
from os import environ
environ["NTFY_USER"] = "your_username"
environ["NTFY_PASSWORD"] = "your_password"
environ["NTFY_TOKEN"] = "your_token"
client = NtfyClient(topic="Your topic")
  1. Send a message:
# Send a message
client.send("Your message here")

docs.title: python_ntfy._ntfy.NtfyClient
docs.text:
NtfyClient(
    topic: str,
    server: str = "https://ntfy.sh",
    auth: tuple[str, str] | str | None = None,
)

A class for interacting with the ntfy notification service.

Itinialize the NtfyClient.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default topic str

The topic to use for this client

required server str

The server to connect to. Must include the protocol (http/https)

'https://ntfy.sh' auth tuple[str, str] | str | None

The authentication credentials to use for this client. Takes precedence over environment variables. Can be a tuple of (user, password) or a token.

None

Returns:

Type Description None

None

Examples:

client = NtfyClient(topic="my_topic")

Action button types.

A view action button.

An HTTP action button.

docs.title: BroadcastAction
docs.text:
BroadcastAction(
    label: str,
    intent: str = "io.heckel.ntfy.USER_ACTION",
    extras: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    clear: bool = False,
)

Bases: Action

A broadcast action button.

The broadcast action sends an Android broadcast intent when the action button is tapped.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default label str

Label of the action button in the notification.

required intent str

Android intent name.

'io.heckel.ntfy.USER_ACTION' extras dict[str, str] | None

Android intent extras.

None clear bool

Clear notification after action button is tapped.

False

docs.title: HttpAction
docs.text:
HttpAction(
    label: str,
    url: str,
    method: str = "POST",
    headers: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    body: str | None = None,
    clear: bool = False,
)

Bases: Action

An HTTP action button.

The http action sends a HTTP request when the action button is tapped.

Initialize an HttpAction.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default label str

Label of the action button in the notification.

required url str

URL to open when action is tapped.

required method str

HTTP method to use for request.

'POST' headers dict[str, str] | None

HTTP headers to send with the request.

None body str | None

HTTP body to send with the request.

None clear bool

Clear notification after HTTP request succeeds. If the request fails, the notification is not cleared.

False

docs.title: send
docs.text:
send(
    self,
    message: str,
    title: str | None = None,
    priority: MessagePriority = MessagePriority.DEFAULT,
    tags: list | None = None,
    actions: list[ViewAction | BroadcastAction | HttpAction]
    | None = None,
    schedule: datetime | None = None,
    format_as_markdown: bool = False,
    timeout_seconds: int = 5,
    email: str | None = None,
) -> dict

Send a text-based message to the server.

Call this function to send a message to the server. The message will be sent to the server and then broadcast to all clients subscribed to the topic.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default message str

The message to send.

required title str | None

The title of the message.

None priority MessagePriority

The priority of the message.

DEFAULT tags list | None

A list of tags to attach to the message. Can be an emoji short code.

None actions list[ViewAction | BroadcastAction | HttpAction] | None

A list of Actions objects to attach to the message.

None schedule datetime | None

The time to schedule the message to be sent.

None format_as_markdown bool

If true, the message will be formatted as markdown.

False additional_topics

A list of additional topics to send the message to.

required timeout_seconds int

The number of seconds to wait before timing out the reqest to the server.

5 email str | None

Forward messages to an email address. Only one email address can be specified.

None

Returns:

Name Type Description dict dict

The response from the server.

Examples:

>>> response = client.send(message="Example message")

>>> response = client.send(message="Example message", title="Example title", priority=MessagePriority.HIGH, tags=["fire", "warning"])

>>> response = client.send(message="*Example markdown*", format_as_markdown=True)

docs.title: send_file
docs.text:
send_file(
    self,
    file: str,
    title: str | None = None,
    priority: MessagePriority = MessagePriority.DEFAULT,
    tags: list | None = None,
    actions: list[ViewAction | BroadcastAction | HttpAction]
    | None = None,
    schedule: datetime | None = None,
    timeout_seconds: int = 30,
    email: str | None = None,
) -> dict

Sends a file to the server.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default file str

The path to the file to send.

required title str | None

The title of the file.

None priority MessagePriority

The priority of the message. Optional, defaults to MessagePriority.

DEFAULT tags list | None

A list of tags to attach to the message. Can be an emoji short code.

None actions list[ViewAction | BroadcastAction | HttpAction] | None

A list of ActionButton objects to attach to the message.

None schedule datetime | None

The time to schedule the message to be sent. Must be more than 10 seconds away and less than 3 days in the future.

None timeout_seconds int

The number of seconds to wait before timing out.

30 email str | None

Forward messages to an email address. Only one email address can be specified.

None

Returns:

Name Type Description dict dict

The response from the server.

Examples:

>>> response = client.send_file(file="example.txt")

docs.title: get_cached_messages
docs.text:
get_cached_messages(
    self,
    since: str = "all",
    scheduled: bool = False,
    timeout_seconds: int = 10,
) -> list[dict]

Get cached messages from the server.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default since str

The timestamp to start from. If set to "all", will return all messages.

'all' scheduled bool

If true, will return scheduled messages.

False timeout_seconds int

The number of seconds to wait for the response.

10

Returns:

Type Description list[dict]

A list of messages.

Examples:

>>> response = client.get(since="all")

>>> response = client.get(since="all", scheduled=True)

>>> response = client.get(since="2019-01-01")

>>> response = client.get(since="2019-01-01", scheduled=True)
